campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear a financial report from the regional transit authority about their investments in March 2026, and voted on a new policy restricting city properties from being used for certain federal activities. The council also discussed staffing challenges and efforts to fill open city jobs and keep current employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New policy limiting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and employee recruitment/retention efforts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a meeting to address hazardous properties that need cleanup. They heard from residents who objected to the county's list of problem properties, voted to approve the abatement work, and also adopted a new policy on technology disruption and a process for residents needing accessibility accommodations.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property cleanups ordered by the county
City council approval of abatement work on identified properties
New Technology Disruption Policy adopted
Process established for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and take action on several maintenance and service agreements. Main decisions included declaring overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance problem that needs to be addressed, approving a report on landscaping and lighting maintenance, and authorizing the Town Manager to sign multi-year service contracts.
keyTopics: Hazardous vegetation and overgrown brush declared a public nuisance
Landscape and lighting maintenance report approved
Multiple five-year service agreements authorized for Town Manager to execute
April 21 meeting minutes approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to prepare for upcoming council sessions, reviewing and organizing agenda items for May 19th and canceling the May 26th draft agenda review. The council also discussed the meeting schedule for fiscal year 2026-2027, made appointments to various city committees and boards, and considered a travel request.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda items for May 19 council meeting
Fiscal year 2026-2027 council meeting schedule approved
City council appointments to committees, boards, and commissions
Travel request approval (Councilmember Foley)

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 provided information about how residents can participate in city government. The council encouraged residents to submit questions before the meeting and explained where to find agendas and meeting locations.
keyTopics: Residents can submit questions in advance using an online form
Meeting locations and agendas posted at least 24 hours ahead
City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street, 3rd Floor) has agenda details available
Contact City Manager's Office at 650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov for agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. The agenda included a salute to the flag and items related to small business support.
keyTopics: Closed-session discussion (confidential matters not disclosed to public)
Small business initiatives or support
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial review session on Cupertino's budget plan for fiscal year 2026-27 to discuss spending priorities and financial planning for the upcoming year. The council also reminded residents and advocates that anyone trying to influence city decisions on laws or policies may need to register as a lobbyist and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review and discussion
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Public testimony and community input on city legislation

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 covered legal disclosure requirements for residents. The council discussed the 90-day deadline that applies if you want to sue or legally challenge any City decisions.
keyTopics: 90-day lawsuit filing deadline for challenging City decisions
Legal disclosure requirements for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 rules

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's spending plans for the next five years and discuss the upcoming budget for 2026-27. They also adopted a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: 5-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) approved
2026-27 operating budget presentation and direction
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All units would be permanently affordable for low-income residents, and the developer is requesting special allowances under state law to make the project financially feasible.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units on a small downtown lot (replacing Lot T parking)
Six-story building on 0.43 acres (19,700 sq ft parcel)
Request for density bonus concessions and waivers under California law
Environmental review underway to determine if project qualifies for streamlined approval
